Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay Judicial Proceedings
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Bernard Alexander, Esq. 310-394-0888 of Alexander Krakow & Glick LLP for Plaintiff Daniel Corral Pulido.
Supreeta Sampath, Esq. 415-231-5855 of the Sampath Law Firm for Plaintiff Daniel Corral Pulido.
Patrick Sherman, Esq. 650-600-5302 of Curely Hessinger & Johnsrud LLP for Defendant ROCKYOU.
Julian Pardo de Zela, Esq. 650-265-0222 of SV Employment Law Firm for Defendant LISA MARINO.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues employment agreement that is signed.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROCKYOU, INC., LISA MARINO'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay Judicial Proceedings.

Defendants RockYou, Inc. and Lisa Marino's petition to compel arbitration is granted and this case is stayed pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings on the claims alleged by plaintiff Daniel Pulido. The incorporation of the AAA Commercial Rules does not constitute clear and unmistakable evidence of an intent to delegate arbitrability to the arbitrator. Notwithstanding federal authority to the contrary, the court chooses to adhere to the well-reasoned and persuasive dicta in Ajamian v. CantorCO2e, L.P. (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 771 on this point. The arbitration agreement is valid and enforceable. RockYou did not need to sign the agreement for it to be a valid agreement since Mr. Pulido signed it in June 2015. Assuming without deciding that the arbitration agreement has some amount of procedural unconscionability, the arbitration agreement is nonetheless enforceable because it is not substantively unconscionable. The agreement is expressly mutual and does not unlawfully require PAGA claims to be arbitrated and, even if it did, that requirement is severable. The administrative relief provision does not unlawfully interfere with the prosecutorial authority of state or federal agencies. The provision clarifies that Mr. Pulido must arbitrate all claims he brings in his personal capacity.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
